learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end execution
learningObjectives.description: Explain complete transportation execution lifecycle from release through settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Understand integrated TM components
learningObjectives.description: Describe how events, exceptions, dashboards, and settlement work together.

learningObjectives.title: Understand execution success factors
learningObjectives.description: Clarify data, integration, tracking, and collaboration factors for reliable execution.

sections.content: Transportation Execution is the complete operational lifecycle that begins after transportation planning and ends with freight settlement. SAP TM coordinates carriers, resources, execution events, monitoring, proof of delivery and settlement to ensure shipments are transported efficiently while maintaining complete business visibility.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Transportation Planning prepares the shipment. Transportation Execution performs, monitors and completes the shipment.

An automotive manufacturer ships engine components from Pune to Chennai. SAP TM releases the Freight Order, confirms the carrier, assigns a truck, captures loading and departure events through GPS integration, monitors shipment progress, records Proof of Delivery at the destination plant and automatically creates the Freight Settlement Document for carrier payment.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Integrate SAP TM with GPS, telematics, mobile applications and EDI-enabled carriers to automate execution events, improve shipment visibility and reduce manual effort.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global manufacturing company integrated SAP TM with its transport management network. More than 95% of shipment events were captured automatically, reducing manual tracking effort by 75% while significantly improving on-time delivery performance.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: For SAP TM certification and interviews, remember the complete execution sequence: Freight Order -> Carrier -> Resource -> Loading -> Departure -> Transit -> Arrival -> Proof of Delivery -> Freight Settlement.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Many learners assume transportation ends after delivery. In SAP TM, the business process is complete only after Proof of Delivery is recorded and Freight Settlement has been processed.

items: Transportation Execution begins after Freight Order creation.
Execution includes carrier confirmation, loading, transit monitoring and delivery.
Event Management and Exception Management ensure shipment visibility.
Proof of Delivery is required before freight settlement.
Freight Settlement completes the end-to-end transportation execution lifecycle.
